vocab.context: Taylor uses 'trophy' to describe the woman, implying that she is seen as a prize or a status symbol by the man.

vocab.definition: to make someone or something less wild or aggressive
vocab.lyric_snippet: Taming a bear, making him care
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'taming' to describe the woman's relationship with the man, suggesting that she is trying to make him more domesticated or loving.

vocab.definition: small cracks or openings in a surface, especially one that is supposed to be solid or watertight
vocab.lyric_snippet: Down in the floorboards, And she just knows she must bolt
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'leaks' to describe the flaws or weaknesses in the relationship, implying that the woman is aware of its instability.

vocab.definition: a vehicle, especially a horse-drawn carriage, used for transportation or ceremonial purposes
vocab.lyric_snippet: The chariot is waitin'
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'chariot' as a metaphor for the relationship, suggesting that it is a vehicle that is waiting to be ridden or controlled.

vocab.definition: breaking or shattering something, especially a relationship
vocab.lyric_snippet: Hearts are hers for the breakin'
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'breakin'' to describe the woman's ability to break or shatter the hearts of those around her, implying that she is a force to be reckoned with.

songs.title: Robin
songs.lyrics:
Long may you reign
You're an animal, you are bloodthirsty
Out window panes talking utter nonsense
You have no idea

Strings tied to levers
Slowed-down clocks tethered
All this showmanship
To keep it for you in sweetness

Way to go, tiger
Higher and higher
Wilder and lighter
For you

Long may you roar
At your dinosaurs
You're a just ruler
Covered in mud, you look ridiculous
And you have no idea

Buried down deep
And out of your reach
The secret we all vowed
To keep it from you in sweetness

Way to go, tiger
Higher and higher
Wilder and lighter
For you

You got the dragonflies above your bed
You have a favorite spot on the swing set
You have no room in your dreams for regrets
(You have no idea)
The time will arrive for the cruel and the mean
You'll learn to bounce back just like your trampoline
But now we'll curtail your curiosity
In sweetness

Way to go, tiger (Way to go, tiger)
Higher and higher (Higher and higher)
Wilder and lighter
For you

vocab.definition: having a strong desire to cause harm or violence
vocab.lyric_snippet: You're an animal, you are bloodthirsty
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe someone who is aggressive and violent, emphasizing their animalistic nature.

vocab.definition: complete or absolute, without any exception or qualification
vocab.lyric_snippet: Out window panes talking utter nonsense
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the nonsense being spoken, emphasizing its complete absurdity.

vocab.definition: the art of presenting something in a way that is impressive or entertaining
vocab.lyric_snippet: All this showmanship
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the presentation or display of something, highlighting its superficial nature.

vocab.definition: fastened or secured with a rope or chain, often to prevent movement or escape
vocab.lyric_snippet: Slowed-down clocks tethered
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the clocks being secured or restrained, emphasizing their limited movement.

vocab.definition: to reduce or limit something, often in a way that is strict or severe
vocab.lyric_snippet: But now we'll curtail your curiosity
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the action of limiting or restricting someone's curiosity, often in a strict or severe manner.

vocab.definition: unfairly or intentionally causing pain or suffering
vocab.lyric_snippet: The time will arrive for the cruel and the mean
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe people who are intentionally causing pain or suffering, emphasizing their unfair or unjust behavior.

vocab.definition: to spring back or recover quickly from a difficult or challenging situation
vocab.lyric_snippet: You'll learn to bounce back just like your trampoline
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the ability to recover or spring back from a difficult situation, often with a sense of resilience or determination.

songs.title: The Manuscript
songs.lyrics:
Now and then, she rereads the manuscript
Of the entire torrid affair
They compared their licenses
He said, "I'm not a donor but
I'd give you my heart if you needed it"
She rolled her eyes and said
"You're a professional"
He said, "No, just a good Samaritan"
He said that if the sex was half as good as the conversation was
Soon, they'd be pushin' strollers
But, soon, it was over

In the age of him, she wished she was thirty
And made coffee every morning in a Frеnch press
Afterwards, she only atе kids' cereal
And couldn't sleep unless it was in her mother's bed
Then she dated boys who were her own age
With dart boards on the backs of their doors
She thought about how he said since she was so wise beyond her years
Everything had been above board
She wasn't sure

And the years passed like scenes of a show
The professor said to write what you know
Lookin' backwards might be the only way to move forward
Then the actors were hitting their marks
And the slow dance was alight with the sparks
And the tears fell in synchronicity with the score
And at last, she knew what the agony had been for

The only thing that's left is the manuscript
One last souvenir from my trip to your shores
Now and then, I reread the manuscript
But the story isn't mine anymore

vocab.definition: extremely passionate or intense, often in a way that is considered scandalous or improper
vocab.lyric_snippet: Of the entire torrid affair
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'torrid' to describe the passionate and intense nature of the relationship.

vocab.definition: a person who gives something, especially money or a part of their body, for a particular purpose
vocab.lyric_snippet: He said, 'I'm not a donor but'
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'donor' to refer to someone who gives a part of themselves, in this case, their heart.

vocab.definition: a person who helps others, especially in an emergency or difficult situation, without expecting anything in return
vocab.lyric_snippet: He said, 'No, just a good Samaritan'
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'Samaritan' to describe someone who is kind and helpful, often in a selfless way.

vocab.definition: a person who is trained and skilled in a particular job or activity
vocab.lyric_snippet: She rolled her eyes and said, 'You're a professional'
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'professional' to describe someone who is skilled and experienced in their field.

vocab.definition: higher or more elevated in position, status, or quality
vocab.lyric_snippet: He said since she was so wise beyond her years, Everything had been above board
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'above' to describe something that is higher in quality or status.

vocab.definition: a flat, usually rectangular, piece of wood or other material used for a particular purpose
vocab.lyric_snippet: Everything had been above board
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'board' to refer to a flat surface or a group of people who make decisions.

vocab.definition: the occurrence of two or more events that seem to be meaningfully connected, but are not necessarily causally related
vocab.lyric_snippet: And the tears fell in synchronicity with the score
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'synchronicity' to describe the connection between the emotions and the music.

vocab.definition: a set of musical notes or a record of the points or marks made in a game or competition
vocab.lyric_snippet: And the tears fell in synchronicity with the score
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'score' to refer to the music and the emotional impact it has on the listener.
